title: Crunchyrolls Timeline Video
wikitext:
[]: Crunchyroll's Timeline Video             Quote Post by iihieiii » Fri Mar 06, 2020 12:48 am Crunchyroll just launched a very nice video about Evangelion Timeline Please i recommend everybody to watch SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: ***Crunchyroll just launched a very nice video about Evangelion Timeline :) ***             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 06, 2020 2:15 am >37 timelines Oh god, this thing. No thanks x 1000. This is nonsense click bait and everyone should stay away from it.

[]: Re: Crunchyroll's Timeline Video             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Fri Mar 06, 2020 2:54 am I can guarantee you that this is complete and utter nonsense and even his coverage of the non-canon merchandising nonsense that Anno himself has stated exists only to make money is innacurate and incomplete. Because I know most of these well enough I even know the specific dark corners of the Internet this guy read in order to produce this awful, awful video, and what he could have researched instead. Because this video is the definition of terrible clickbait. And I will do all of that sometime later this weekend when I have the time. Just please, please don't take this video even remotely seriously.

[]: Re: Crunchyroll's Timeline Video             Quote Post by MuscleRobo » Fri Mar 06, 2020 9:10 am FelipeFritschF wrote: I can guarantee you that this is complete and utter nonsense and even his coverage of the non-canon merchandising nonsense that Anno himself has stated exists only to make money is innacurate and incomplete. Because I know most of these well enough I even know the specific dark corners of the Internet this guy read in order to produce this awful, awful video, and what he could have researched instead. Because this video is the definition of terrible clickbait. And I will do all of that sometime later this weekend when I have the time. Just please, please don't take this video even remotely seriously.

[]: Re: The idea of a timeloop             Quote Post by AWinters » Mon Aug 24, 2020 8:20 pm I've just spent the past hour watching a YouTube video about the "37 evangelion timelines". Of course it's a fan theory, but worth watching. The guy draws a map of what he believes is the Evangelion multiverse, including every comic book, every video game, every obscure product ad placement - you name it! Hell, just watch it for the comedy value. It's hard not to cringe at all the Evangelion stuff some of us didn't know existed. The theory is that everything is canon because Kaworu is a multiverse traveller (apparently, he found references to this in all Eva medias). I'm torn between taking it as a joke and believing that Anno would make the answer that crazy.

[]: Re: Crunchyroll's Timeline Video             Quote Post by glitz2hard » Tue Aug 25, 2020 11:59 am it's a very INTERESTING and ENTERTAINING theory. and it's clearly based in humor and intended to be humorous. if you can sit through it, it's not a bad video, and the Kaworu thing is DEFINITELY something worth thinking about. Purely because of how lame it is that he still hasn't had success yet.

[]: Re: Crunchyroll's Timeline Video             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Aug 30, 2020 6:18 pm glitz2hard wrote: it's a very INTERESTING and ENTERTAINING theory. and it's clearly based in humor and intended to be humorous. if you can sit through it, it's not a bad video, and the Kaworu thing is DEFINITELY something worth thinking about. Purely because of how lame it is that he still hasn't had success yet.

[]: Re: Crunchyroll's Timeline Video             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Aug 31, 2020 9:02 am ^ you should never evaluate art by the means of how other people might perceive it. The video is kinda dumb, and the tone of it is kinda cringeworthy, but in the end it's harmless, imo.
